title: sorghum bicolor var. subglabrescens
intro: Sorghum bicolor var. subglabrescens, also known as broom sorghum, is a type of plant that belongs to the sorghum species and is characterized by its small stature and slender stems. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is valued for its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have health benefits such as lowering cholesterol levels and reducing the risk of certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of broom sorghum, including infusions and decoctions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
